template_usage: The Ifrit - Jinn Death Spirit. Import with: php tools/repeatable/php/database-tools/import_supernatural_entities.php path/to/this_file.json
appearance: Young man with crew-cut hair and slightly caricatured, overly-military bearing and manner. Has numerous bullet-wounds in back and torso which slowly trickle blood. When readying to kill or defend, takes on terrifying aspect: smokeless flame leaps from mouth, black horns curl from forehead, and claws form at the end of fingers.
biography:
According to the Qur'an, the Ifrit are infernal Jinn and the most powerful Jinn to walk the earth. They are death spirits, who dwell in Jahannam, the underworld where the wicked and evil are punished. However, they are easily drawn to Earth, as they feed on the life force and blood of murder victims. Once having done so, they take on the physical appearance of the murder victim at the point of their death and are compelled to hunt down the murderer. Once they have killed the murderer, the Ifrit can chose to remain on earth or return to Jahannam. If they remain on Earth, they are compelled to inhabit desolate places, like uninhabited caves or ruins.

This particular Ifrit was drawn to Earth in Iraq. A platoon of soldiers, searching a town for insurgent fighters, was ambushed. Half the platoon's members died in the ambush, but they eventually prevailed. The unit commander, a very green second lieutenant, survived, but lost his mind and demanded that the townsfolk were rounded up and executed for the crime of not warning the soldiers of the ambush. When his men refused, the lieutenant first threatened them with a court-martial, before drawing his sidearm and threatening to shoot his own troops. In response, two of the men fired their rifles, wounding the officer. After a brief consultation, all the remaining men gathered up the AK rifles of the enemy and shot the lieutenant at the same time, so that they were all complicit. They then reported their commander killed by enemy fire.

As with any Jinn, an Ifrit can be either a believer or an unbeliever, good or evil. The Ifrit that rose to sup upon the blood and fading life-force of the dying second lieutenant is most definitely both an unbeliever and a wicked, ruthless, and evil being. It stalked the lieutenant's murderers, killing the first two (the initial shooters) while they were still in Iraq. When the rest of the soldiers transferred out, the Ifrit followed them. The creature stalks its victim slowly, terrorizing its target before killing them. It has killed all but one of the soldiers and the man in question knows quite well that all his old squadmates are dead. He is unaware what is hunting him, but he knows, without a shadow of a doubt, that his platoon was cursed back there in that town whose name he's forgotten.

The Ifrit uses Essence to power its Disciplines and can use it to heal wounds in the same way as vampires use Vitae. It regains all Essence and heals all damage (even Aggravated) at nightfall. The Ifrit can soak all damage with its full Stamina + Fortitude and is immune to fire, mind control and emotional influences, though not the madness of Dementation.

A faithful Muslim can cause an Ifrit to discorporate and return to Jahannam by invoking Allah in a short Du'a (prayer of supplication). Likewise, a Christian can force an Ifrit to flee by crossing himself and a Jew can make an Ifrit flee by invoking King Solomon. In all these cases, True Faith is not needed, but the person in question must actually be a follower of the religion in question.

When not actively seeking out its quarry or terrorizing the target, the Ifrit will dwell in a ruined house, abandoned factory, or other desolate place.
notes:
THE IFRIT - INFERNAL JINN DEATH SPIRIT

WHAT IT IS:
- Infernal Jinn from Jahannam (Islamic Hell)
- Death spirit that feeds on murder victims
- Takes form of victim at point of death
- Compelled to hunt and kill murderer
- Can choose to stay on Earth or return after mission complete

ORIGIN STORY:
- Drew to Earth in Iraq
- Fed on dying US 2nd Lieutenant
- Lieutenant was murdered by his own men (friendly fire)
- Platoon executed him after he threatened them
- Ifrit now hunting all who participated

CURRENT STATUS:
- Has killed all but one soldier
- Final target knows all squad mates are dead
- Target knows something is hunting him
- Doesn't know what or how to stop it
- Living in terror

APPEARANCE:
- Normal: Young military man with bullet wounds
- Combat: Flames from mouth, horns, claws
- Wounds constantly trickle blood
- Military bearing and manner
- Terrifying when transformed

HUNTING BEHAVIOR:
- Stalks victim slowly
- Terrorizes before killing
- Patient and methodical
- Enjoys the fear
- Will eventually kill unless stopped

POWERS:
- Uses Essence (20 pool)
- Equivalent to Auspex 2, Celerity 1, Fortitude 3, Potence 3, Thaumaturgy 3
- Soaks all damage with Stamina + Fortitude
- Regenerates all Essence and health at nightfall
- Immune to fire
- Immune to mind control and emotional influence
- NOT immune to Dementation (madness affects jinn)

RELIGIOUS VULNERABILITIES:
- Faithful Muslim: Du'a prayer forces discorporation
- Faithful Christian: Crossing self causes Ifrit to flee
- Faithful Jew: Invoking King Solomon causes flee
- Does NOT require True Faith
- Person must genuinely follow that religion
- Ifrit returns to Jahannam when banished

HABITAT:
- Dwells in desolate places
- Ruined houses, abandoned factories
- Avoids populated areas except when hunting
- Cannot rest in holy ground

ALIGNMENT:
- This Ifrit is EVIL and an UNBELIEVER
- Wicked and ruthless
- Enjoys causing terror
- Some Ifrit are good (this one isn't)

powers.name: Heightened Senses
powers.description: Can sense surroundings with supernatural clarity - equivalent to Auspex 2

powers.name: Supernatural Speed
powers.description: Enhanced speed and reflexes - equivalent to Celerity 1

powers.name: Infernal Durability
effects: Soaks all damage with Stamina + Fortitude

powers.name: Lure of Flames
powers.description: Control over fire - equivalent to Thaumaturgy Path of Flames 3
effects: Breathe fire when in combat form

power_pool.regeneration: Regenerates ALL Essence at nightfall. Also heals all damage (including aggravated) at nightfall.

special_abilities.name: Nightfall Regeneration
special_abilities.description: At nightfall, regenerates all Essence and heals all damage including aggravated. Essentially unkillable unless banished.

special_abilities.name: Murder Victim Form
special_abilities.description: Takes the appearance of murder victim at point of death. Shows fatal wounds. Compelled to hunt murderer.

special_abilities.name: Desolate Dwelling
special_abilities.description: When not hunting, must dwell in desolate, abandoned places. Cannot rest in populated areas or holy ground.

special_abilities.name: Religious Banishment
special_abilities.description: Can be banished by faithful followers of Abrahamic religions (Muslim, Christian, Jewish) through specific religious actions.

immunities: Most forms of lasting harm (regenerates at nightfall)
vulnerabilities: Jewish invocation of King Solomon (causes fleeing)
Compelled to dwell in desolate places
Compelled to hunt murderer (cannot ignore mission)

relationships.description: The murder victim whose form the Ifrit took. US military officer killed by his own men in Iraq.
The soldiers who murdered the lieutenant. Ifrit has killed all but one. Compelled to hunt them all.
The last surviving member of the platoon. Knows he's being hunted but doesn't know by what.

custom_data.form: Appears as murder victim at point of death

essence_mechanics.healing: Can spend to heal like vitae
essence_mechanics.bonus: ALL damage (even aggravated) heals at nightfall

religious_banishment.muslim: Du'a (prayer of supplication) invoking Allah - Ifrit discorporates and returns to Jahannam
religious_banishment.christian: Crossing self while faithful - Ifrit must flee
religious_banishment.jewish: Invoking King Solomon - Ifrit must flee
religious_banishment.requirements: Must be genuine follower of religion

behavioral_patterns.patience: Will take days/weeks to build terror
behavioral_patterns.dwelling: Inhabits ruined house in industrial area

custom_data.special_notes: The Ifrit is nearly unkillable through violence (regenerates everything at nightfall). Religious banishment is the primary solution. Characters might: protect the final target, learn about Jinn from occult sources, find faithful person of Abrahamic religion to banish it, or try to reason with it (unlikely to work with evil Ifrit). High-Humanity Muslims/Christians/Jews in the party become crucial. Creates interesting religious subplot - does the target deserve to die for murder?

actingNotes: Play the Ifrit as terrifying but with military bearing. It speaks (though rarely) with the lieutenant's voice and mannerisms. When terrorizing, it appears with wounds bleeding, staring without blinking. When in combat form, describe the transformation: mouth opening impossibly wide, flames erupting, bones cracking as horns emerge. It doesn't explain or monologue - it just hunts. The only time it speaks is to say things like 'You should have let them live' or 'Judgment has found you.' Voice should be emotionless, matter-of-fact. The terror comes from inevitability, not theatrics.
agentNotes: The Ifrit works best if a character IS the final target, or cares about someone who is. Creates moral dilemma: the target is a murderer (friendly fire, but still). Does he deserve to die? Can they protect him? The Ifrit is nearly impossible to kill (full regeneration at nightfall). Violence is futile. Solution requires: research (learn about Jinn), religious help (find faithful Muslim/Christian/Jew), and moral choice (protect murderer or let justice happen?). Ifrit can also threaten anyone who helps target - will terrorize and attack protectors. High-Occult characters can research. Religious characters become crucial. Creates tension between justice and mercy.
health_status: Supernatural being - regenerates fully at nightfall
